claim: Hematopoietic progenitor cells are never susceptible to HIV-1 infection ex vivo.

result.claim: Hematopoietic progenitor cells are never susceptible to HIV-1 infection ex vivo.
result.reasoning: The searches for propositions and chunks related to "Hematopoietic progenitor cells are susceptible to HIV-1 infection ex vivo", "Hematopoietic progenitor cells are not susceptible to HIV-1 infection ex vivo", "HIV-1 infection of human hematopoietic stem and progenitor cells ex vivo", "susceptibility of CD34+ cells to HIV-1 ex vivo", and "human CD34+ hematopoietic progenitor cells resistant to HIV-1 ex vivo" did not yield any direct evidence to support or refute the claim. The retrieved propositions and chunks were either about other types of progenitor cells, general aspects of HIV infection, or unrelated topics. There is no specific information found in the knowledge base that directly addresses the susceptibility or resistance of hematopoietic progenitor cells to HIV-1 infection ex vivo.
